steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries (punctuation) question. The skill is joining two independent clauses correctly. The clause before the blank (“Since 1989, Indianapolis's Eiteljorg Museum has celebrated the work of groundbreaking contemporary Native artists”) and the clause after (“in 2013, it awarded Buffalohead one of its prestigious artist fellowships”) are both grammatically complete. The blank must supply punctuation that properly connects them without a coordinating conjunction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — artists, thus: A comma followed by “thus” (a conjunctive adverb) creates a comma splice — a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses. A semicolon is required before a conjunctive adverb linking two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice B — artists,: A comma alone between two independent clauses is a comma splice, which violates Standard English boundary conventions. ✗
  • Choice C — artists and: “And” is a coordinating conjunction, but it requires a comma before it when joining two independent clauses (“artists, and”). Without the comma, artists and does not conform to Standard English conventions. ✗
  • Choice D — artists;: A semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses without a coordinating conjunction, making this the only one that conforms to Standard English boundary rules. ✓
That is the correct answer.
